stemHtml: An operator has a fleet of reciprocating-engine aeroplanes for commercial air operations. Unless destination alternate fuel is carried, the operator should use a destination aerodrome as an isolated aerodrome if the alternate fuel plus the FRF that is required to reach the nearest adequate destination alternate aerodrome is more than the amount of fuel required to fly either for (1) _____ or for (2) _____, whichever is less.

options.a: (1) 30 minutes plus 10% of the flying time planned for cruising, including FRF; (2) 2 hours
options.b: (1) 30 minutes plus 10% of the flying time planned for cruising, including FRF; (2) 1 hour
options.c: (1) 45 minutes plus 15% of the flying time planned for cruising, including FRF; (2) 2 hours
options.d: (1) 45 minutes plus 15% of the flying time planned for cruising, including FRF; (2) 1 hour
